vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1562

plan.title: bug(triage): REST-batched cache bootstrap still spends minutes in default local write delay

narratives.Description: The triage bootstrap path already retrieves issues through REST pagination, but the local cache write loop still sleeps by default. This story removes the multi-minute intentional delay from normal bootstrap runs and adds progress plus regression coverage so operators can tell work is moving.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

task triage:bootstrap now uses the REST-paginated cache fetch path, but the default runtime is still much slower than expected because cache_fetch_all keeps the old per-entry delay behavior during the local cache write loop.

A live maintainer run on 2026-06-09 completed successfully but took about 388 seconds. The output showed populate_cache wrote 432 issues and skipped 39 fresh entries. The GitHub retrieve path should only need a handful of REST list pages at per_page=100, so the duration is not explained by remote issue retrieval.

## Observed behavior

- task triage:bootstrap spent several minutes in step 1 populate_cache with no intermediate progress.
- Final recap: issues_written=432 already_fresh=39 issues_failed=0.
- Total elapsed time: about 387.9s.

## Code path checked

- scripts/triage_bootstrap.py delegates to cache.cache_fetch_all.
- scripts/cache.py delegates fetch-all to _cache_fetch.run_fetch_all.
- scripts/_cache_fetch.py uses rest_issue_list_paginated for enumeration, so issue retrieval is REST-paginated rather than per-issue gh issue view.
- scripts/cache.py still sets DEFAULT_DELAY_MS = 500.
- _cache_fetch.run_fetch_all calls _maybe_sleep(delay_ms) after each issue write and again at every batch_size checkpoint.

For 432 writes with the current defaults, the intentional sleep budget alone is roughly (432 + 43) * 0.5s = 237.5s before local JSON rendering, scanning, and disk writes are counted.

## Why this matters

The #1239 REST migration appears to have fixed the remote retrieval shape, but the old pacing defaults still make normal bootstrap feel hung. The UX is especially poor because populate_cache emits only start/done progress, so operators cannot tell whether the job is retrieving, sleeping, scanning, or stuck.

## Suggested fix

- Make the default delay 0 for the REST-paginated path, or only sleep on actual rate-limit/retry paths.
- Add progress inside run_fetch_all for long cohorts, for example every N entries written/skipped.
- Update stale comments/tests that still describe task scm:issue:view as the live implementation path.
- Add a test that protects the production default from reintroducing multi-minute sleeps on REST-batched fetches. Current scale tests pass delay_ms=0, so they do not catch the slow default.

## Acceptance criteria

- A default task triage:bootstrap run for a few hundred open issues no longer spends minutes in intentional local sleeps.
- The cache fetch path remains REST-paginated at per_page=100 and does not regress to per-issue GitHub reads.
- Operators see progress during populate_cache on large cohorts.
- Stale references to task scm:issue:view in this path are corrected or clearly marked historical.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update scripts/cache.py and scripts/_cache_fetch.py so the default REST-paginated cache fetch path does not sleep after every local issue write and emits periodic populate_cache progress for large cohorts.
2. Add pytest verification evidence in tests/test_cache_fetch.py and tests/test_triage_bootstrap.py so production defaults stay delay-free for REST-batched fetches, REST pagination remains in use, and stale task scm:issue:view references are corrected or explicitly historical.
narratives.UserStory: As a Deft operator, I want triage bootstrap to avoid intentional local sleeps and show progress, so that large cache refreshes no longer look hung for minutes.
narratives.Traces: Issue #1562 maintainer timing report; scripts/cache.py DEFAULT_DELAY_MS; scripts/_cache_fetch.py run_fetch_all.

items.title: A default task triage:bootstrap run for a few hundred open issues no longer spends minutes in intentional local sleeps.

narrative.Acceptance: Given task triage:bootstrap uses the default cache_fetch_all settings, when the REST-paginated path writes a few hundred issues, then the production default does not add per-entry local sleep delays.

items.title: The cache fetch path remains REST-paginated at per_page=100 and does not regress to per-issue GitHub reads.

narrative.Acceptance: Given the cache fetch path enumerates issues, when tests run, then they verify rest_issue_list_paginated remains the enumeration path and per-issue gh issue view is not reintroduced.

items.title: Operators see progress during populate_cache on large cohorts.

narrative.Acceptance: Given run_fetch_all processes a large cohort, when progress thresholds are reached, then populate_cache emits written/skipped/failed progress so operators can distinguish active work from a hang.

items.title: Stale references to task scm:issue:view in this path are corrected or clearly marked historical.

narrative.Acceptance: Given comments or tests describe the cache fetch implementation, when the story is complete, then stale task scm:issue:view references are removed or explicitly marked as historical rather than current behavior.

expected_outputs: cache fetch tests assert delay-free REST-batched defaults, progress output, and no per-issue GitHub read regression

references.title: Issue #1562: bug(triage): REST-batched cache bootstrap still spends minutes in default local write delay
